channel.title: SOC Intelligence Digest
channel.description: Seven-day operational threat intelligence and IOC reporting for SOC review. Coverage window: 2026/07/09 16:50 - 2026/07/16 16:50 JST. Article dates: 2026/07/09 - 2026/07/16 JST.

item.title: Qantas Did Everything “Right” — And Got Breached Anyway. Regulators Say That’s the Point.
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Malware, Threat Actor, Identity, Data Breach, AI Security, Network, Incident Response / Topics: Network Edge, Vulnerability, Malware, Identity, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (77) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: A vishing call to an overseas contact center agent. A fake IT ticket. A default setting nobody thought to lock down. That's all it took to expose the personal data of roughly 5 million Australians — and now the country's privacy regulator has decided Qantas isn't to blame for it. The Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC) closed the book this week on its year-long preliminary inquiry into the June 2025 Qantas data breach , and the conclusion cuts against the instinct to punish the victim of a cyberattack. Also read: Australia’s Qantas Confirms Cyberattack: 6 Million Service R / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/threat-actor/

item.title: Nichirei Cyberattack Hits KFC Japan, Disrupts Frozen Food Supply
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Ransomware, Supply Chain, Data Breach, Mobile, Network / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware, Supply Chain, Malware, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (83) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN Japan/Asia relevance, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, Japan related, critical sector, primary references 2 / Summary: The Nichirei cyberattack has disrupted food deliveries across Japan after the frozen food and logistics provider confirmed its servers were compromised in a cybersecurity incident involving unauthorized access. The attack affected logistics operations supporting KFC Japan, forcing temporary service disruptions while the company investigates the incident and works to restore systems. Nichirei Corporation said it detected system failures on July 13 and established an emergency response headquarters the same day. "Nichirei Corporation (the "Company") experienced system failures on July 13, 2026, / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/knowledge-hub/what-is-a-cyber-attack/

item.title: インシデント対応の舞台裏シリーズ：サプライチェーン攻撃編
item.description: Source: LAC / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Supply Chain, Incident Response / Topics: Supply Chain / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, JA attack activity, JA critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, Japan source, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: 企業が日常的に信頼を寄せるベンダーが起点となって、標的企業のシステムへ侵入する「サプライチェーン攻撃（supply chain attack）」を取り上げます。ある中堅製造業A社をモデルケースに、サプライチェーン攻撃の発覚から収束、事後対応まで時系列に沿ってどのようにインシデント対応を行うかご紹介します。 / Primary source: LAC https://www.lac.co.jp/lacwatch/service/20260716_004949.html / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery

This analysis breaks down the attack chain, payload delivery, and recommended defenses. The post Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog . / Primary source: Microsoft Security Blog https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/07/15/unpacking-asyncapi-npm-supply-chain-compromise-import-time-payload-delivery/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Rapid7 MDR Team Discovers New SonicWall SMA1000 Zero Days being Actively Exploited (CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410)
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Patch, Mobile, Endpoint, Network, Detection / Topics: CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410, Microsoft, Network Edge, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 2 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, IOC 13, linked IOC evidence, coherent attack event, target and impact context, vulnerability exploitation context, reported by 2 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: Overview On July 14, 2026, SonicWall published a security advisory addressing two vulnerabilities affecting SMA1000 Series remote access appliances, including the critical server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability CVE-2026-15409 (CVSS 10.0) and the high-severity code injection vulnerability CVE-2026-15410 . The advisory urges customers to immediately apply the latest platform hotfix releases. Successful exploitation of CVE-2026-15409 permits an unauthenticated attacker to open a websocket-based tunnel to arbitrary localhost-only services, while CVE-2026-15410 is a local privilege escala / Also reported by: Tenable / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/etr-rapid7-mdr-team-discovers-new-sonicwall-sma1000-zero-days-being-actively-exploited-cve-2026-15409-cve-2026-15410 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: ClaudeFix: Shared Claude Chats Meet ClickFix
item.description: Source: Zscaler / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Threat Actor, Data Breach, AI Security, Detection, Cryptocurrency, Application Security / Topics: Apple/iOS, Malware, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, IOC 209, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, primary references 1 / Summary: IntroductionClickFix is a widely employed attack technique, first seen in 2024, where a victim is instructed to paste-and-run instructions on their system to “fix” a problem or install software. The seemingly benign instructions are, in fact, malicious and lead to the deployment of malware onto the victim’s system. Zscaler Threat Hunting has identified recent ClickFix attacks abusing Anthropic’s Claude platform through the use of shareable Claude chats to host these instructions, which marks a shift from typical attacks. As AI platforms have grown in popularity, threat actors have increasingly / Primary source: Zscaler https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/claudefix-shared-claude-chats-meet-clickfix / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: The Risk of Exposed Cloud Functions and How to Harden
item.description: Source: Google Cloud Threat Intelligence / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Exploit, Threat Actor, Cloud, AI Security, Application Security / Topics: Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: Written by: Corné de Jong Introduction Mandiant security assessments frequently identify publicly exposed serverless applications that lack authentication, often as a result of specific business requirements. Serverless deployments typically run custom-developed code that incorporates third-party packages, making them targets for a wide range of application-level attacks, including: Local and Remote File Inclusion (LFI/RFI) Command Injection Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities can grant an attacker full control over the underlying container instance. Such access can serve as a foo / Primary source: Google Cloud Threat Intelligence https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/exposed-cloud-functions-harden/ / Primary source age: 0 days

This complex framework employs TookPS, exfiltrates seed phrases, monitors Chromium-based browsers, and installs various malware strains, including the Rilide stealer. / Primary source: Securelist https://securelist.com/okobot-framework-targets-cryptocurrency-wallets/120660/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Daxin Returns: Stealthy Malware Resurfaces in Taiwan Alongside a New Backdoor
item.description: Source: Security.com Threat Research / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Detection, APT, Threat Actor / Topics: Malware, APT / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: APT / targeted campaign (rank 6) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: APT / targeted campaign, EN attack activity, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, EN Japan/Asia relevance, EN critical sector, APT/campaign priority, IOC 2, coherent attack event, Japan related, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: Four years after Symantec first uncovered Daxin, the most advanced malware we had seen from a China-linked actor, it has been found running inside a Taiwan manufacturing firm, deployed with a novel new backdoor. / Primary source: Security.com Threat Research https://www.security.com/threat-intelligence/daxin-returns-stupig / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: CISA Adds SonicWall SMA1000 Vulnerabilities to KEV Catalog Following Active Exploitation
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intel, Exploit, Patch, Network, Detection, Incident Response, Application Security / Topics: Network Edge, Vulnerability, Exploit / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (83) / Focus: active exploitation (rank 4) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: active exploitation, EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, active exploitation priority, target and impact context, vulnerability exploitation context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context, indicator context, primary references 2 / Summary: Security researchers have identified two critical vulnerabilities, CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, affecting SonicWall SMA1000 Series appliances. The flaws are already being exploited in the wild, prompting urgent warnings from SonicWall and CISA. Successful exploitation could result in Remote Code Execution, bypass of security restrictions, and broader compromise of affected systems. The vulnerabilities impact SonicWall SMA1000 models 6210, 7210, and 8200v running versions 12.4.3-03245, 12.4.3-03387, 12.4.3-03434 (platform-hotfix), 12.5.0-02283, 12.5.0-02624, and 12.5.0-02800 (platform-hot / Primary source: psirt.global.sonicwall.com https://psirt.global.sonicwall.com/vuln-detail/SNWLID-2026-0008

item.title: Three Russians Indicted in $62M Cybercrime Scheme Targeting U.S. Infrastructure
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intel, Malware, Phishing, Cloud, DDoS, Network, Detection, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware, Malware, Phishing, Cloud / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (73) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, critical sector, primary references 2 / Summary: Three Russian nationals have been charged in a sweeping Russian cybercrime indictment tied to an alleged bulletproof hosting operation that U.S. authorities say enabled ransomware , malware , phishing , and other cybercriminal activities, resulting in more than $62 million in losses to victims across the United States and several other countries. The U.S. Attorney's Office for the Northern District of Ohio announced the unsealing of the indictment following a seven-year investigation. Alongside the criminal charges, the U.S. Department of State is offering a reward of up to $10 million for inf / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/general/

item.title: 侵害までわずか6分：「Patriot Bait」の攻撃者はいかにAIを使ってC&Cボットネットを構築・展開したか
item.description: Source: Trend Micro / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: AI Security, Threat Intel, Malware, Threat Actor, Incident Response / Topics: AI Security, Malware / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, campaign priority, IOC 4, linked IOC evidence, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, Japan source, primary references 1 / Summary: TrendAI™ Researchは、ロシア語話者の攻撃者がAIを使って稼働中のボットネットを運用していたことを示す、200件を超えるGemini CLIのセッションログを分析しました。攻撃者自身が担った作業はわずか11%で、C&Cの全面移行を6分で完了していました。 / Primary source: Trend Micro https://www.trendmicro.com/ja_jp/research/26/g/actor-behind-patriot-bait-used-ai-to-deploy-c2-botnet.html / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: 7-Zipの改変版インストーラーを介したレジデンシャルプロキシ化についてまとめてみた
item.description: Source: piyolog / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Exploit, Malware, Threat Actor, Cloud, Data Breach, Endpoint, Network, Application Security / Topics: Apple/iOS, Linux, Microsoft, Network Edge, Exploit, Malware, Cloud, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: APT / targeted campaign (rank 6) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: APT / targeted campaign, EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, JA attack activity, JA IOC/detection/hunting, JA active exploit/PoC/zero-day, JA detection/analysis, JA targeted/APT/state-backed, JA Japan/Asia relevance, APT/campaign priority, IOC 4, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan related, primary references 1 / Summary: 2026年1月頃、圧縮ソフト「7-Zip」を装った非公式サイトで配布されていた偽インストーラーを実行すると、端末がレジデンシャルプロキシのノードとして悪用される事案を、国内外のセキュリティベンダーや研究者らが相次いで報告しました。Infobloxは2026年7月、この活動の背後にいる攻撃者を「Lurking Lizard」と呼称し、WireVPNを含む複数のブランドを長期にわたり使い分けてきたとする分析を公表しています。ここでは関連する情報をまとめます。 Lurking Lizardとは何か 非公式サイトを通じて7-Zipになりすました改変版インストーラーを配布し、実行した端末をレジデンシャルプロキシのノードとして悪用する手口である。マルウェア自体は研究者のLuke Acha氏が命名した「upStage Proxy」という呼称があり(出典: 3)、Infobloxはこの手口を長期にわたり展開してきた攻撃者を「Lurking Lizard」と呼称している(出典: 7)。両者は指し示す対象のレイヤーが異なり、「upStage Proxy」はマルウェア(挙動)の呼称、「Lurking Lizard」は運営主体(アクター)の呼称として扱われる。 Infobloxの観測によれば、Lurking Lizardは2026年1月に表面化した7-Zip偽装キャンペーンにとどまらず、WireVPNを含 / Primary source: piyolog https://piyolog.hatenadiary.jp/entry/2026/07/14/225515 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Nihon Kotsu Cyberattack Disrupts Japan’s Largest Taxi Operator
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Data Breach, Network / Topics: Network Edge, Malware, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (79) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 2 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN Japan/Asia relevance, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, Japan related, reported by 2 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: The Nihon Kotsu cyberattack has disrupted operations at Japan's largest taxi operator after the company confirmed that its internal systems were compromised by a malware-related security incident. The cyberattack on Nihon Kotsu forced the company to shut down parts of its IT infrastructure, leaving key Japan taxi service operations, including its taxi dispatch system, unavailable. According to the company, the incident occurred early on Saturday, July 11, 2026. After detecting unauthorized access, Nihon Kotsu immediately shut down affected systems to contain the attack and prevent additional d / Also reported by: Security Affairs / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/announcement/

item.title: CrashStealer: New macOS Infostealer Uses Signed Apps to Evade Gatekeeper

Jamf Threat Labs first spotted CrashStealer in early May 2026 as a suspicious macOS sample uploaded to VirusTotal. By early July, in-the-wild detections confirmed the malware had moved from development into active deployment. The malware is […] / Primary source: jamf.com https://www.jamf.com/blog/crashstealer-macos-infostealer-analysis/

item.title: US Treasury Sanctions VPN Provider Linked to Ransomware
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Patch, Malware, Ransomware, Data Breach, Network / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware, Malware, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (73) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, critical sector, primary references 2 / Summary: The U.S. Treasury Department has announced new ransomware sanctions against a virtual private network (VPN) provider, its administrator, and a malware service provider accused of enabling ransomware attacks targeting Americans. The Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) said the designated individuals and entity allegedly supplied infrastructure and tools used by cybercriminals to carry out attacks against U.S. businesses, hospitals, financial institutions, and critical infrastructure. The action, coordinated with the United Kingdom, is part of broader efforts to disrupt the cybercrime ecosys / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/cybercrime/fraud/

item.title: 個人用リポジトリで見つかった米国セキュリティ機関の認証情報についてまとめてみた
item.description: Source: piyolog / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Vulnerability, Exploit, Threat Actor, Cloud, Identity, Data Breach, Incident Response / Topics: Cloud Security, Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud, Identity, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, JA critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan source, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: 2026年7月9日、米国のサイバーセキュリティ・インフラストラクチャセキュリティ庁(CISA)は、委託先担当者(以下「当該担当者」)の個人用GitHubリポジトリを通じて同庁のAWS認証情報などが長期間インターネット上に公開状態にあった事案について、対応の経緯と教訓をまとめた記事を公表しました。ここでは関連する情報をまとめます。 外部からの通報で発覚 GitGuardianの自動監視システム「Public Monitoring」は2026年5月13日までに、当該担当者が保有する個人用GitHubリポジトリ「Private-CISA」を検知し、同社の自動プログラム「Good Samaritan」がコミット作成者宛てに9通の通知メールを送信していた。(出典: 3) 同社の研究者Guillaume Valadon氏は5月14日にリポジトリの内容を精査し、「最初は偽物(でっちあげ)ではないかと思うほど酷い内容だった」としつつ本物と確認し、CERT/CCポータル経由で現地時間5月14日16時14分(CET)にCISAへ通報した。(出典: 3, 4) CERT/CCからは自動応答のみで週末を控えていたため、GitGuardianは5月15日朝、あえてBrian Krebs氏(KrebsOnSecurity)に連絡し、同氏のCISA人脈を通じて情報を転送してもらうことを決めた。(出典: 3) K / Primary source: piyolog https://piyolog.hatenadiary.jp/entry/2026/07/13/123024 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: マルウェアValleyRATの感染を狙った日本を標的とするスパムメール
item.description: Source: Cybereason / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Threat Intel / Topics: Malware / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, JA Japan/Asia relevance, campaign priority, IOC 16, coherent attack event, detection evidence context, Japan related, primary references 1 / Summary: KEY POINTS ValleyRATに関する攻撃について、Cybereason GSOCでは、偽インストーラを起点とする攻撃とスパムメールを起点とする攻撃の2種類が確認されている。 今回取り上げるスパムメールの攻撃について、中国語話者と日本語話者の両方を標的としていると思われ、マルウェアには解析妨害や検知回避などが確認された。 Cybereason GSOCにおけるValleyRATの検知手法について一例を解説した。 INTRODUCTION ValleyRAT Threat Intelligence 今回のテーマであるマルウェアValleyRATは2023年にProofpoint社により […続きを読む] / Primary source: Cybereason https://www.cybereason.co.jp/blog/spider-labs/levelblue-spiderlabs-blog/14327/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Software developers are the target. New trojan attacks supply chains and inflicts multifaceted damage on infected PCs
item.description: Source: Dr.Web / Source attributes: Russia / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Supply Chain, Endpoint / Topics: Microsoft, Supply Chain, Malware / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, JA attack activity, campaign priority, IOC 3, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: A new trojan engaging in supply chain attacks has recently come under the scrutiny of our antivirus laboratory. The malware primarily targets C++ and C# project files. This malicious sample is particularly dangerous as its payload incorporates multiple damaging features, allowing it to steal data, access clipboard content, operate as a backdoor, engage in rogue mining. and also infect other files. First discovered in the last quarter of 2025, the malware has been updated and upgraded by its makers ever since. It mainly spreads over the Internet via infected executable files and Python scripts. / Primary source: Dr.Web https://news.drweb.com/show/?i=15276&lng=en&c=5 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: 未成年者らのグループによる快活CLUBへの不正アクセスについてまとめてみた
item.description: Source: piyolog / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: AI Security, Vulnerability, Exploit, DDoS, Data Breach / Topics: AI Security, Vulnerability, Exploit, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, JA active exploit/PoC/zero-day, JA detection/analysis, JA Japan/Asia relevance, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, vulnerability exploitation context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context, Japan related, primary references 1 / Summary: 2025年1月、複合カフェ「快活CLUB」等を運営する株式会社快活フロンティアは、自社サーバーへの不正アクセスにより会員の個人情報が外部に漏えいした可能性があると公表しました。その後の警視庁の捜査により、SNSでつながった10代を中心とするグループが、対話型AI「ChatGPT」を悪用して自作した攻撃プログラムを用いて大量の不正アクセスを行っていたとみられることが明らかになり、2025年12月以降、複数の未成年者・元未成年者が不正アクセス禁止法違反などの容疑で相次いで逮捕・書類送致されています。ここでは関連する情報をまとめます。 会員情報729万件の漏えい可能性 快活フロンティアは2025年1月18日(土)夕刻、自社サーバーへの不正アクセスを検知し、直ちにサーバーをネットワークから切り離すなどの対策を実施した。その後、外部専門機関の助言を受けながら影響範囲等の調査を進める過程で、会員アカウントを管理するシステムへの不正アクセスの形跡を確認した(出典: 1, 2)。 1月20日にはDDoS攻撃によるネットワーク障害の発生を確認。快活フロンティアは快活CLUBアプリの機能を「会員証の表示」のみに制限した。同日、対策本部を設置して会員アプリサービスの利用制限を開始し、外部への第一報を公表した(出典: 1)。 1月21日、快活フロンティアと親会社の株式会社AOKIホールディングスは、外部か / Primary source: piyolog https://piyolog.hatenadiary.jp/entry/2026/07/09/222807 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: マイクロソフト社よりセキュリティ更新プログラム（2026年7月）が公開されました
item.description: Source: Canon MJ ESET Security / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Vulnerability, Exploit, Patch, Cloud, Endpoint / Topics: Cloud Security, Microsoft, Malware, Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud / Priority: Critical (93) / SOC: 73 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, campaign priority, coherent attack event, Japan source, primary references 1 / Summary: マイクロソフト社より、7月15日 にセキュリティ更新プログラムの情報が公開されました。 今回公開されたプログラムではセキュリティ面での深刻度「緊急」のものが10件含まれています。 今後のこれらの脆弱性を悪用したマルウェアなどによる攻撃に備え、早期にセキュリティ更新プログラムの適用を推奨いたします。今回のセキュリティ更新プログラムで修正した脆弱性には、更新プログラムが公開されるよりも前に悪用が行われているものが含まれていますので、早急に更新プログラムの適用を行ってください。 【プログラム一覧】 以下の情報は、マイクロソフト社のページからのものです。 2026 年 7月のセキュリティ更新プログラム (月例) 更新プログラムの詳細はマイクロソフト社のページでご確認下さい。 最大深刻度 製品ファミリ 最も大きな影響 緊急 Windows 11 v26H1, v25H2, v24H2, v23H2 リモートでコードが実行される 緊急 Windows Server 2025 (Server Core installationを含む) リモートでコードが実行される 緊急 Windows Server 2022 (Server Core installationを含む) リモートでコードが実行される 緊急 Windows Server 2019, 2016 (Server Core install / Primary source: Canon MJ ESET Security https://eset-info.canon-its.jp/malware_info/news/detail/260715.html / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Investigating Persistence Mechanisms in AWS

While your environment scales and changes in seconds, adversaries are embedding themselves into your IAM policies, Lambda functions, and federated sessions, creating invisible footholds that survive long after you believe an incident is closed. Persistence in AWS is not just a technical oversight; it is a fundamental business risk. If you cannot see how an attacker has rooted themselves in your environment, you cannot contain them. item.title: サイバー攻撃によるニチレイグループのシステム障害についてまとめてみた
item.description: Source: piyolog / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach / Topics: Ransomware, Data Breach / Priority: High (74) / SOC: 62 / Confidence: High (88) / Focus: incident / breach (rank 3) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: incident / breach, JA attack activity, JA Japan/Asia relevance, target and impact context, uncertain reporting, Japan related, primary references 1 / Summary: 2026年7月13日、株式会社ニチレイは、同社グループのシステムが不正アクセスによる障害を受け、冷蔵倉庫の入出庫業務や冷凍食品の出荷業務に影響が生じていると公表しました。その後、同社のサーバーがサイバー攻撃を受けたことを確認したとして続報を公表し、障害の影響は取引先・関連組織の商品供給にも及んでいます。ここでは関連する情報をまとめます。 障害は冷蔵倉庫の入出庫業務にも影響 ニチレイは持株会社であり、事業会社として食品事業を担うニチレイフーズと、低温物流事業を担うニチレイロジグループなどを傘下に持つ(出典: 14)。株式会社ニチレイは2026年7月13日、「当社グループでのシステム障害発生について」を公表し、不正アクセスによるシステム障害が発生したことを明らかにした(出典: 1)。ニチレイの広報は取材に対し、社内のシステム部門からの報告でシステム障害を確認したのは同日午前6時50分ごろだったと説明している(出典: 6, 7)。 システム障害の影響は、ニチレイロジグループ各社が担う冷蔵倉庫の入出庫業務と、ニチレイフーズの冷凍食品出荷業務に及び、現時点では障害範囲は国内に限定されるとした(出典: 1)。ニチレイは発生同日に緊急対策本部を設置し、顧客・取引先の個人情報や顧客データの保護を最優先に、グループで使用するシステムの遮断措置を講じたことを、7月15日公表の第2報で明らかにした(出典 / Primary source: piyolog https://piyolog.hatenadiary.jp/entry/2026/07/16/022032 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: My Stack Simulator, (Wed, Jul 8th)
item.description: Source: SANS ISC / Source attributes: Global / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel / Priority: High (73) / SOC: 51 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: The stack is a memory region where a program stores temporary data -&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;like local variables and return addresses. Think of the stack as a pile of plates in your kitchen: you can only add a new plate to the top, and you can only take one away from the top too. Programs use this same "last in, first out" principle to keep track of what they&#;x26;#;39;re doing. Every time a function is called, the program pushes a new plate onto the stack containing things like local variables and the address to return to once the function finishes. When the function is done, that plate is / Primary source: SANS ISC https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/33138 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Finance phishing works because it sounds boringly normal
item.description: Source: Help Net Security / Source attributes: Croatia / News / Secondary / Categories: Security, Threat Intel, Exploit, Phishing, Threat Actor, AI Security / Topics: Exploit, Phishing, AI Security / Priority: High (63) / SOC: 66 / Confidence: Medium (46) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector, primary source not found / Summary: Finance departments process a constant stream of invoices, contracts, payment notices, and procurement emails, making email one of the most common initial access vectors for threat actors. According to Cofense, attackers exploit those workflows with phishing emails that resemble legitimate business correspondence rather than relying on urgency-based lures. Such phishing emails are also likely to bypass AI-based secure email gateways (SEGs) and other email security technologies.
